Supercut · creative playbook

coffee

What the longest-running paid social video ads in this market keep doing — counted, with the footage to prove it.

2026-07-12 · 54 ads studied across 48 brands

How we picked these ads

Advertisers kill losing ads fast, so an ad still delivering after a month has earned a look. We searched 240 ads in this market — including its heaviest spenders, so the pool reflects where real budgets go, not just what got views — and kept the ones running 30+ days and seen in the last 30. An AI watched each surviving ad and answered a fixed set of questions about it; code then counted the answers. 14 ads that turned out to sell something else were rejected — they are listed at the bottom. No single ad is called a winner here. The findings are patterns that repeat across 54 ads from 48 different brands.

What the studied ads sell

brewing equipment25 of 54
beans & pods13 of 54
mushroom coffee5 of 54
beverage containers2 of 54
drinkware2 of 54
7 one-off segments7 of 54

Top takeaways

50 of 54Show the product within the first three seconds of the video.
29 of 54Prioritize a creator feel over polished brand assets.
53 of 54Design every video to work perfectly without any sound.
  1. Show the full process to build trust product demo · 19 ads
  2. Use energetic creators for honest reviews creator testimonial · 12 ads
  3. Solve a common coffee frustration quickly ugc problem-solution · 12 ads
  4. Create an aspirational mood with quick clips lifestyle montage · 7 ads
  5. Keep it simple with bold text on screen still image with text · 4 ads
  6. What to test next
  7. What to double-check

Chapter 1 · product demo

Show the full process to build trust

19 ads 18 brands 201 median days live 4 median cuts in first 10s 27s median length

This format lives on Facebook: 18 of 19 ads.

Clean demonstrations are the most common way to sell coffee gear. These ads often feature energetic music and fast pacing to keep viewers engaged.

The first three seconds, side by side

How these ads open

visual hook10 of 19
problem callout2 of 19
bold claim2 of 19
social proof2 of 19
question1 of 19
offer or discount1 of 19
curiosity tease1 of 19

When the product first appears

first 3 seconds19 of 19

How long they are

under 15s6 of 19
15-30s6 of 19
30-60s6 of 19
over 60s1 of 19

How they ask for the click

hard9 of 19
none5 of 19
soft5 of 19

The button under the ad

Shop now14 of 19
Learn more3 of 19
Order now2 of 19

How to shoot it

  • Show the product immediately in the first three seconds. (19 of 19 ads)
  • Record a full start to finish demonstration of the product. (16 of 19 ads)
  • Use a visual hook to grab attention at the start. (10 of 19 ads)
  • Ensure the video makes sense even if the volume is muted. (19 of 19 ads)

Examples that lasted

Lastaforest
Opens with: “Transform inconsistent tamping into barista-level precision.” · 165 days live · 135k plays · watch the ad
nothing.eco
Opens with: “HOT OR COLD?” · 161 days live · 398k plays · watch the ad
Izzat Express Global
Opens with: “The portable coffee maker doesn't exist...” · 159 days live · 24k plays · watch the ad

Watch out

Almost no one in this group uses a simple question as their hook.

Chapter 2 · creator testimonial

Use energetic creators for honest reviews

12 ads 10 brands 196.5 median days live 5.5 median cuts in first 10s 36s median length

Runs on Facebook (9) and TikTok (3).

Creators help humanize the brand by talking directly to the camera. These videos tend to run longer but maintain a steady pace.

How to shoot it

  • Keep the feel authentic to the creator rather than the brand. (11 of 12 ads)
  • Instruct the creator to maintain an energetic emotional tone. (7 of 12 ads)
  • Film a full demo of them using the coffee or equipment. (11 of 12 ads)
  • Use a medium pacing for the edit to keep it natural. (11 of 12 ads)

Examples that lasted

Alex Home Barista
Opens with: “If you don’t want to spend more than 200 bucks on an espresso machine, keep watching.” · 290 days live · 1.2M plays · watch the ad
Viscount LLC
Opens with: “So I headed into Target to grab this highly sought out Stanley cup and create a commercial with it.” · 229 days live · 48 plays · watch the ad
Meraki Tech Global
Opens with: “I got a new espresso machine and it's probably the most innovative machine I've used.” · 221 days live · 800k plays · watch the ad

Watch out

Only one ad in this group recorded audio that is actually required for the viewer to understand the message.

Chapter 3 · ugc problem-solution

Solve a common coffee frustration quickly

12 ads 11 brands 193.5 median days live 5 median cuts in first 10s 36s median length

This format lives on Facebook: 10 of 12 ads.

These ads focus on fixing a specific issue the viewer has with their routine. They use very fast pacing to show the transition from problem to result.

How to shoot it

  • Open with a clear callout of the problem being solved. (5 of 12 ads)
  • Use a hard call to action asking the viewer to buy. (8 of 12 ads)
  • Include a full demonstration of the solution in action. (10 of 12 ads)
  • Maintain high energy throughout the entire video. (10 of 12 ads)

Examples that lasted

Jared Brubaker
Opens with: “If you can't focus or remember things,” · 194 days live · 95k plays · watch the ad
RYZE Superfoods
Opens with: “Guys, is this the best Black Friday deal of the year?” · 193 days live · 10k plays · watch the ad
Gustosa
Opens with: “First man on the moon? Na, first coffee on the moon.” · 228 days live · 783 plays · watch the ad

Watch out

Only one creator in this set used humor to address a coffee problem.

How to shoot it

  • Use aspirational imagery to set the emotional tone. (3 of 7 ads)
  • Ensure the brand feel is the primary focus of the edit. (6 of 7 ads)
  • Show only a glimpse of the product instead of a full demo. (5 of 7 ads)
  • Keep the video duration between fifteen and thirty seconds. (4 of 7 ads)

Chapter 5 · still image with text

Keep it simple with bold text on screen

4 ads 4 brands 230 median days live 0 median cuts in first 10s 9s median length

This format lives on Facebook: 4 of 4 ads.

Only 4 ads fit this format — read this chapter as a hint, not a rule.

Simple images can work if the text is strong enough. These are usually the shortest ads in the pool.

Shop now2 of 4

How to shoot it

  • Keep the video length under fifteen seconds. (4 of 4 ads)
  • Use a hard call to action in the overlay or button. (3 of 4 ads)
  • Show the product on the screen immediately. (4 of 4 ads)
  • Use a slow pace as there are no cuts to manage. (3 of 4 ads)

Examples that lasted

229 days live · 40k plays · watch the ad

Homyfyty
Opens with: “Brew Comfort at Home” · 231 days live · 53 plays · watch the ad

Watch out

None of these ads use a creator feel; they are all strictly brand-led.

What to test next

The First-Second Product Shot

Hypothesis
If we show the product in the very first second instead of just the first three, we will increase viewer retention.
Why this test
50 out of 54 ads show the product in the first 3 seconds, making it a market standard.
Call it on
Retention at 3 seconds
Shot notes
  • Product must be the center of the frame.
  • Use bright lighting on the logo or packaging.
  • Remove any introductory logos before showing product.
Risk
The opening may feel too much like a commercial and cause immediate scrolling.
Draft script
See this perfect crema? This is why our beans matter. Shop the roast now.

Problem-Solution with Humor

Hypothesis
If we use a humorous script to call out a coffee problem, we will stand out from the energetic and serious ads.
Why this test
Only 1 out of 12 ugc problem-solution ads used a humorous tone.
Call it on
Click-through rate
Shot notes
  • Creator should exaggerate the struggle with old coffee gear.
  • Use funny sound effects for the 'problem' phase.
  • Keep the 'solution' phase high energy and clean.
Risk
The joke may not land and could distract from the product benefits.
Draft script
Is your current brew basically bean water? Stop hurting yourself. Upgrade your morning.

Silent Demo with Bold Text

Hypothesis
If we use large text overlays to explain the demo, we will win over the muted-video audience.
Why this test
53 out of 54 ads are designed to work with the sound off.
Call it on
Conversion rate
Shot notes
  • Text must cover 20% of the screen area.
  • Match text timing exactly to the physical action in the demo.
  • Highlight a bold claim in the first overlay.
Risk
Too much text can clutter the screen and hide the product.
Draft script
BETTER COFFEE. 30 SECONDS. ZERO MESS. Get your kit today.

Sample brand brief

This section shows the brief generator on a made-up brand: “Bloom, a small-batch mushroom coffee brand for busy parents”. In the app, you type your own brand and the tests below are rewritten for it.

Bloom is a small-batch mushroom coffee brand designed to support the energy needs of busy parents.

Bloom can dominate the mushroom coffee niche by adopting the high-retention product demo formats used by market leaders. By focusing on energetic tones and quick pacing, the brand can stand out among the 5 mushroom coffee ads currently in market.

The Instant-Impact Product Hook

The idea
Show the Bloom packaging within the first second to anchor the brand immediately. This ensures busy parents identify the product before they can scroll away.
The pattern it leans on
50 out of 54 ads show the product in the first 3 seconds, making it a market standard.
Opening line
“The new morning ritual for exhausted parents is finally here.”
Draft script
[0-2s] Close-up of Bloom bag being opened. [2-10s] Quick cuts of the powder dissolving instantly. [10-15s] A tired parent takes a sip and looks refreshed. [15s+] Text: Shop Bloom Small-Batch.

Humorous Paren-Hood Struggles

The idea
Use a lighthearted creator testimonial that jokes about the survival needs of parenting. This differentiates Bloom from the serious tone of most competitors.
The pattern it leans on
Only 1 out of 12 ugc problem-solution ads used a humorous tone.
Opening line
“I used to be a person, now I'm just a walking zombie fueled by bad caffeine.”
Draft script
[0-5s] Creator looking messy with laundry, holding a 'regular' coffee. [5-15s] Quick swap to a clean kitchen with Bloom. [15-25s] Explanation of mushroom energy without the jitters. [25s+] Click the link for your parent survival kit.

Silent Ritual Text Overlay

The idea
A fast-paced demo of the brewing process designed specifically for muted mobile viewing. Large text overlays will communicate the unique mushroom benefits clearly.
The pattern it leans on
53 out of 54 ads are designed to work with the sound off.
Opening line
“CLEAN ENERGY. NO JITTERS. PARENT PROOF.”
Draft script
[0-3s] Large text: BETTER THAN REGULAR COFFEE. [3-12s] Fast clips of spooning Bloom into a mug and adding water. [12-20s] Text overlay: FOCUS FOR THE SCHOOL RUN. [20-27s] Shot of the Bloom bag with 'Shop Now' button.

What to double-check

Everything above comes from an AI watching ads one at a time and code counting its answers. Here is where that process was noisy, and what it threw out.

Ads rejected as off-market (14)

The search also returned ads selling something else. They were dropped before any counting, so they do not touch the numbers above.

AdvertiserWhy it was dropped
Milky Plantoff-market: sells "A countertop water filtration and instant boiling system."
Clean Simple Eatsoff-market: sells "Clean Simple Eats vanilla protein powder for use in coffee recipes."
Becoming the Ultimate Human with Gary Breckaoff-market: sells "A dietary supplement called Perfect Amino."
Site To Buyoff-market: sells "A service that sets up a private-label Shopify coffee dropshipping store for you."
Go Brewingoff-market: sells "A non-alcoholic coffee stout beer containing roasted coffee beans."
addallxroff-market: sells "Addall XR, a nootropic dietary supplement designed to increase mental focus."
Site To Buyoff-market: sells "A service that builds custom Shopify coffee dropshipping stores, including design, branding, and sourcing."
Sophie's dealsoff-market: sells "It sells a 12'x16' hardtop backyard gazebo."
Luna's Mushroomsoff-market: sells "Lion's Mane mushroom powder intended to be added to coffee or other drinks for stress relief and focus."
FocusBlinkoff-market: sells "Mushroom focus strips for brain support."
Luke the PT Dadoff-market: sells "A portable home sauna that uses red and infrared light."
Vorioaloff-market: sells "Women's professional workwear/lifestyle apparel, specifically a blazer and trousers set."
AF Inventionsoff-market: sells "Custom wooden coasters and bottle openers made with a 3D printer and laser cutter."
At-home Gym Cluboff-market: sells "A whole-body vibration plate for exercise and health benefits."

Ads we could not analyze (1)

These never reached the questionnaire, so they are absent from every count.

AdvertiserWhat went wrong
addallxrvideo download failed (curl exit 22)

How reliable was the watching?

We measured the editing pace of every ad twice: once by the AI's eye, once by scene-detection software. The AI was off by 2.4 cuts on average, and within 2 cuts of the software on 59% of ads. The cut counts printed in the chapters are the software's, not the AI's.

Where a spoken hook could be checked against the ad library's own transcript (20 ads), the AI's quote matched 20 times and disagreed 0 times. The other 34 ads had on-screen-text hooks, no hook, or no transcript — nothing to check against.

10 of 54 ads were described by the AI as making no ask, yet the ad unit itself carries a purchase button (ads f3ceb491247f019424154, fe0ae602e19999a020122, fdf35298e62c9e0accb1b, f49ffb94892baea95ba28, f7b7f015392c915b8d6ff, f3363d3be4d9d65afc492, f4ffda667dbf9e12cee57, afc8513033f430404a49, ff5bf764d6056e9db82fc, fe9bd9fc3912b313915b0). Read those "no ask" counts with that in mind: the AI judges the video, not the button under it.

The AI reported firm format labels for every ad in the pool.

Assumptions this report makes

  • "Still delivering" means the ad was seen in the ad library within the last 30 days. The data offers no direct "currently live" flag.
  • Days-running and play counts come from the ad library and are taken at face value.
  • A long-running ad earned study, but nothing here proves any single ad was profitable. Only the repeated patterns are conclusions.
  • Spoken quotes were transcribed by the AI from the audio; where the ad library had its own transcript, we cross-checked (see above).

Study ranking: ads are ordered for the example lists by a score of longevity ×0.5 + engagement ×0.3 + formatConfidence ×0.2, each part normalized within this pool, with at most one ad per brand in any list. Engagement is measured as a rate — likes and shares per play, a share counting 5× a like — because plays on a paid ad are bought, so raw counts would mostly rank budgets. Rates from under 10,000 plays are ignored as noise.

Made with Supercut. Days-running and play counts come from the ad library data; "still delivering" means seen in the last 30 days, the closest signal the data offers to "currently live".
